faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Corona home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, silken tubes in fabric folds, and small beige or golden moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small caterpillars) on clothing or shed larval casings. If you spot any of these signs in your Corona home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What is the typical cost of clothes moth control in Corona, Queens?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Corona typically range from $550 to $5,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of the affected area, and the treatment method required. Minor infestations in single rooms cost less, while whole-home treatments or severe cases requiring multiple visits fall at the higher end. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes for all Corona residents.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work in Corona homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically eliminates adult moths and larvae within 24-48 hours, but complete eradication requires 2-4 weeks as eggs hatch and residual treatments take effect. Most Corona homes require 2-3 follow-up visits spaced 2-3 weeks apart to ensure all life stages are eliminated. We'll also provide guidance on cleaning and prevention to protect your garments long-term.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths in Corona spread to my neighbors' apartments?
faqs.answer: While clothes moths don't typically migrate between apartments as readily as other pests, they can spread through shared laundry facilities, storage areas, or if infested items are moved between units. In Corona's multi-family buildings, we recommend notifying your building management if you discover an infestation so neighboring units can be inspected, helping prevent re-infestation and protecting the entire building.
